published.text:
### Hero
Heading: AI Voice Agent vs. Call Center
Body: Outsourced call centers have been the go-to solution for small businesses that need phones covered. But AI voice agents now handle the same job at a fraction of the cost — with better CRM integration, consistent scripts, and zero hold times. This page compares both options honestly for small service businesses.
Sub: Below: monthly costs, per-call pricing, call quality, CRM integration, and a decision framework.

### Core sections
- Two approaches to never missing a call (cards: Call center, AI voice agent, Hybrid)
- Side-by-side comparison (table: monthly cost, per-call cost, availability, script accuracy, CRM integration, scalability)
- When each approach makes sense (when-to-use)
- Hidden costs of call centers (considerations: hold times, script drift, turnover, integration gaps)
- Case studies referencing existing projects

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers about AI voice agents vs. call centers

### Final CTA
Heading: Thinking about switching from your call center?
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will compare your current call center costs and call quality to what an AI voice agent could deliver.

edits.description: Published as part of batch 4. Targets businesses currently paying for call center services.
Interlinks to AI phone answering, AI voice agent for restaurants, AI appointment setter.
Provides real cost ranges ($300-$2K/mo for call center vs $200-$500/mo for AI).
Honest about call center advantages for complex multi-topic calls.
